sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.66 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: For a group$G$, let$D_n(G)$denote the$n$-th dimension subgroup of$G$, and$_n(G)$the$n$-th term of its upper central series. For a given integer$n 1$, let$f(n)= \{ m \; \;G\; \;n\; \;D_m(G) 1\}$and$g(n)= \{ m\ |\ \ \ G\ \ n\ \ D_n(G) _m(G)\}.$a) What is$f(3)$? b) (B.\,I.\,Plotkin). Is it true that$f(n)$is finite for all$n$? c) Is the growth of$f(n)$and$g(n)$polynomial, exponential, or intermediary?...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
